# full-example

A realistic agent-policy config that exercises every renderer and every attribution option.

## Project overview

This repo is a worked example of an agent-policy setup. It demonstrates
pinned sections (included everywhere), renderer-targeted sections,
and full vs. concise output modes.

## Conventions

- Prefer small, focused modules.
- Tests live alongside the code they test.
- No TODO graveyards. Delete stale comments.

## Detailed workflow

This section is long enough that it should only live in the full
AGENTS.md document. It explains the branching strategy, review
expectations, and how to recover from merge conflicts. Concise
renderers (CLAUDE.md, copilot-instructions.md) omit it.

## Copilot tips

Short, imperative sentences work best for Copilot's instruction
file. Avoid long explanations or references to external documents.

## Attribution

All commits must end with an `Assisted-by:` footer.

- AI-assisted: `Assisted-by: {agent}:{model}`
- Human-only: `Assisted-by: n/a`
- Multiple footers are allowed for multi-vendor work.
